author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. x. 16, ‘Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves,’ etc. Delivered on a Festival of Martyrs.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. x. 16, ‘Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves,’ etc. Delivered on a Festival of Martyrs.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Holy Gospel was read, Brethren, ye heard how our Lord Jesus Christ strengthened His Martyrs by His teaching, saying, “Behold, I send you forth as lambs in the midst of wolves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now consider, my Brethren, what he does.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If but one wolf come among many sheep, be they ever so many thousands, they will all be put to confusion by one wolf in the midst of them: and though all may not be torn, yet all are frightened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What manner of design is this then, what manner of counsel, what manner of power, not to let in a wolf amongst the sheep, but to send the sheep against the wolves!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I send you,” saith He, “as sheep in the midst of wolves;” not to the neighbourhood of wolves, but “in the midst of wolves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There was then at that time a herd of wolves, and but few sheep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the many wolves killed the few sheep, the wolves were changed and became sheep.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us hear then what advice He hath given, who hath promised the crown, but hath first appointed the combat; who is a spectator of the combatants, and assisteth them in their toil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What manner of conflict hath He prescribed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Be ye,” saith He, “wise as serpents, and simple as doves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso understandeth, and holdeth to this, may die in assurance that he will not really die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For no one ought to die in this assurance, but he who knows that he shall in such sort die, as that death only shall die in him, and life be crowned.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore, Beloved, I must explain to you, though I have often spoken already on this subject, what it is to be “simple as doves, and wise as serpents.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if the simplicity of doves be enjoined us, what hath the wisdom of the serpent to do in the simplicity of the dove?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This in the dove I love, that she has no gall; this I fear in the serpent, that he has poison.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now do not fear the serpent altogether; something he has for thee to hate, and something for thee to imitate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when the serpent is weighed down with age, and he feels the burden of his many years, he contracts and forces himself into a hole, and lays aside his old coat of skin, that he may spring forth into new life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Imitate him in this, thou Christian, who dost hear Christ saying, “Enter ye in at the strait gate.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Apostle Paul saith to thee, “Put ye off the old man with his deeds, and put ye on the new man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast then something to imitate in the serpent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Die not for the “old man,” but for the truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso dies for any temporal good dies “for the old man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when thou hast stripped thyself of all “that old man,” thou hast imitated the wisdom of the serpent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Imitate him in this again; “keep thy head safe.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what does this mean, keep thy head safe?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Keep Christ with thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have not some of you, it may be, observed, on occasions when you have wished to kill an adder, how to save his head, he will expose his whole body to the strokes of his assailant?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would not that that part of him should be struck, where he knows that his life resides.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And our Life is Christ, for He hath said Himself, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here the Apostle also; “The Head of the man is Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso then keepeth Christ in him, keepeth his head for his protection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what need is there to commend to you in many words the simplicity of the dove?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the serpent’s poison had need to be guarded against: there, there was a danger in imitation; there, there was something to be feared; but the dove may you imitate securely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark how the doves rejoice in society; everywhere do they fly and feed together; they do not love to be alone, they delight in communion, they preserve affection; their cooings are the plaintive cries of love, with kissings they beget their young.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea even when doves, as we have often noticed, dispute about their holes, it is as it were but a peaceful strife.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do they separate, because of their contentions?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nay, still do they fly and feed together, and their very strife is peaceful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See this strife of doves, in what the Apostle saith, “If any man obey not our word by this epistle, mark that man, and have no company with him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Behold the strife; but observe now how it is the strife of doves, not of wolves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He subjoined immediately, “Yet count him not as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The dove loves even when she is in strife; and the wolf even when he caresses, hates.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore having the simplicity of doves, and the wisdom of serpents, celebrate the solemnities of the Martyrs in sobriety of mind, not in bodily excess, sing lauds to God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He who is the Martyrs’ God, is our Lord God also, He it is who will crown us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we shall have wrestled well, we shall be crowned by Him, who hath crowned already those whom we desire to imitate.
